ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ka Jakob yɛl ye, “M sɔsidif nɛ, yɛlimi fʋ yʋ'ʋr!” Ka o lɛbis ye, “Ka bɔ ka fʋ bu'osidi m yʋ'ʋrɛ?” Ka niŋ o zug bareka anina.
translated: Jacob said, "I am tired, let us rest!" Then he asked, "What shall we eat when we rest?" Then he went and gathered some leaves.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ala ka Wina'am da maal ninsaal nwɛnɛ o mɛŋi an si'em la. Dau nɛ pu'a ka o da maal.
translated: The woman went to the farm to work, but she was tired and sat down.

ref: years old, he had three sons and named them Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text: ) la, o da du'a Sem nɛ Ham nɛ Jafet.
translated: Yes, they are the descendants of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Ka maliak la yɛl o ye, “Haga onɛ an Sarai yammʋga, fʋ yi yaanɛ na, ka wa'ae yaanɛ?” Ka o gandig ye, “M zɔtnɛ yiti m zugdaan Sarai san'an.”
translated: He asked her, “Why did Sarai say that you should not go, what did she say?” She replied, “I don’t know what Sarai said to me.”

ref: and they had a son.
text: ka o tʋk pʋʋgʋ du'a biribiŋ.
translated: He went to buy some food.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ka Jakob yɛl ye, “M sɔsidif nɛ, yɛlimi fʋ yʋ'ʋr!” Ka o lɛbis ye, “Ka bɔ ka fʋ bu'osidi m yʋ'ʋrɛ?” Ka niŋ o zug bareka anina.
translated: Jacob said, "I am tired, let me rest a while!" Then he asked, "Where can I find a place to rest?" Then he went and sat under a tree.
